lifecycle.operator_decision_summary: Operator 2026-07-02 ordered per-MT drift spot-checks with adversarial sub-agent review; no MT has been validated by an official validator. Wave-B audit: set_event_emitter has zero production callers - the emitter is NEVER installed in the shell, so every LIVE-wired emit call site (rich save, rich undo, route-to-stage) is a guaranteed no-op; zero editor events reach any transport outside tests; implementation_result overstates. FlightRecorderPane mounted nowhere and the only FlightRecorderQuery impl is the test mock - the observability pane cannot be opened. The typed backend blocker is REAL and verified (FR ingestion endpoint rejects native editor events: deny_unknown_fields, closed 3-variant enum, hardcoded actor_id, UUID session_id) - backend ingestion endpoint is new backend work to carry into a backend-scoped MT. Fix: install emitter at shell startup, mount FR pane + production query impl or record AC-7 blocked, carry backend ingestion endpoint. One-event-ledger leg of CX-003-VIS currently 0 percent functional live.
lifecycle.remediation_appendage: Install the event emitter at shell startup (all emits currently no-op); mount FlightRecorderPane with a production query impl or record AC-7 blocked; backend FR ingestion endpoint carried to MT-109.

acceptance_criteria: After saving a rich document via the native editor, at least one FlightEvent with action='document_saved' and schema='hsk.native_editor@0.1' appears in GET /api/flight_recorder filtered by actor_id='native_editor_human' — proven by integration test against real PG.
After a canvas node placement, at least one FlightEvent with action='canvas_node_placed' appears in the flight recorder — proven by integration test.
After an undo_fired event, at least one FlightEvent with action='undo_fired' appears in the flight recorder — proven by integration test.
emit() failures (e.g. backend unreachable) do not crash the egui frame or block the UI; the error is silently logged to the in-memory error ring — proven by unit test simulating a failed POST.
surface_extension_seam.rs compiles with cargo check (trait stubs are valid Rust, no unused-import errors beyond the allowed dead_code suppress) — proven by cargo check in CI.

EditorSurfaceRegistry with a registered mock surface receives on_selection_changed and on_event_emitted callbacks during a SharedSelection change and a document_saved emit respectively — proven by unit test.
FlightRecorderPane widget loads and displays at least one native editor event when one exists in the backend — proven by kittest after an integration-test event is emitted.
cargo test -p handshake-native event_emitter -- --nocapture passes with no failures.
proof_targets: cargo test -p handshake-native event_emitter -- --nocapture  =>  all PASS
cargo check -p handshake-native  =>  surface_extension_seam.rs compiles without errors
integration test (real PG): save rich doc, GET /api/flight_recorder?actor=native_editor_human, assert 'document_saved' event present
integration test: canvas node place, GET flight recorder, assert 'canvas_node_placed' event present
unit test: failed POST in emit() => error added to error ring, no panic, no egui frame block
kittest: open FlightRecorderPane, assert at least one 'fr-event-*' ListItem present after document save
implementation_notes: POST /api/flight_recorder/runtime_chat_event is the correct endpoint — confirmed at api.ts line ~2339 (the React emitRuntimeChatEvent function). Body must be RuntimeChatEventV0_1 (api.ts line ~607): include schema_version='hsk.fr.runtime_chat@0.1', event_id (UUID), ts_utc (RFC3339), session_id, wsid. The native_editor_event payload goes in a nested JSON field.
tokio::spawn for emit: the app must have a tokio runtime. If the native app uses a different async executor, adapt accordingly. Use a oneshot channel to log the result back to the error ring.

The EditorSurface trait uses object safety rules: all methods take &self (not self), use Box<dyn EditorSurface>. Avoid generic methods in the trait; use concrete types (SharedSelection, NativeEditorEvent) to preserve object safety.

FlightRecorderView.tsx (React reference): imports getEvents (api.ts line 2316) with FlightEventFilters. The native FlightRecorderPane uses the same endpoint. Filter eventType='system' and use actor filter to isolate native editor events.

React reference for event types: FlightEvent.event_type (api.ts line 574) shows 'system' is the appropriate type for native editor events — do not invent 'native_editor' as a new event_type; the backend schema is closed.

Cross-WP dependency on WP-011: this MT reuses the EXISTING WP-011 shared bus (event_bus.rs + command_registry.rs), the EXISTING WP-011 canvas_board.rs, the EXISTING WP-011 backend_client.rs HTTP client, and the EXISTING WP-011 accessibility/* modules for AccessKit wiring. These WP-011 modules must already be present in src/frontend/handshake_native/src/ before this MT runs.

REAL EMITTER, WIRE-WHERE-LIVE / DEFER-WHERE-UNMOUNTED (KERNEL_BUILDER gate 2026-06-23 - the MT-035 anti-scaffolding lesson): the NativeEditorEventEmitter + emit_* helpers + schema must be REAL and WIRED at call sites that EXIST + are tested NOW: (a) emit_document_saved hooks the rich-text SAVE path (MT-020 rich_editor/save/save_manager.rs - that path is live + tested); (b) emit_undo_fired hooks the undo dispatch (MT-035 wired the rich-pane undo live - emit there); (c) emit_route_to_stage hooks the MT-033 route-to-stage command. For emit call sites in panes NOT yet mounted in app.rs (code-edit batch, canvas placement live path) - HONESTLY DEFER to E11/MT-069 (carry-forward), do NOT add a test-only emit helper that no live code calls (that is the MT-035 unwired-scaffolding trap the panel will flag). Each emit_* helper is unit-testable STANDALONE (correct body shape) regardless; the LIVE wiring follows the pane mount. Record an E11 carry for the deferred emit call sites.

surface_extension_seam.rs IS CONTRACTUALLY DESIGN-ONLY (KERNEL_BUILDER gate - legitimate, NOT the scaffolding the no-mockup rule forbids): the contract EXPLICITLY scopes this file as design-only Rust trait stubs (EditorSurface + EditorSurfaceRegistry) that COMPILE but have no runtime path, with #[allow(dead_code)] + a module doc-comment stating it is a design seam. This is a documented extension-seam design (the WP's 'future surfaces attach without touching the emitter' requirement), DISTINCT from hidden unwired scaffolding masquerading as a live feature. KEEP it design-only + clearly documented; the EditorSurface trait MUST be object-safe (all &self, Box<dyn>, no generics/associated-types, RISK-3/MC-3 - write the future method sigs as comments + verify object-safety). The PANEL should hold the EMITTER to real-wiring but ACCEPT the seam as contractual design-only (call this out so the correctness lens does not mis-flag the seam).

VERIFY FLIGHT-RECORDER SHAPE + BOUNDED SPAWN (KERNEL_BUILDER gate - MT-022/029 route lesson + RISK-1/2): VERIFY read-only POST /api/flight_recorder/runtime_chat_event + the RuntimeChatEventV0_1 body (schema_version='hsk.fr.runtime_chat@0.1', event_id, ts_utc, session_id, wsid) + GET /api/flight_recorder against src/backend/handshake_core (Flight Recorder is a real kernel observability system). event_type='system' is CLOSED - the native_editor payload NESTS inside the event payload; do NOT invent a 'native_editor' event_type (RISK schema, MC). serde body MUST be exact snake_case (RISK-1/MC-1 - unit-test build_post_body asserts every required field). emit() is OFF the egui frame thread (tokio::spawn) BUT BOUNDED by a Semaphore(permits=20) - if saturated, DROP + log to the cap-20 in-memory error ring, never unbounded task accumulation (RISK-2/MC-2). actor_id: verify the backend actor-validation rules; use a valid format like 'hsk:native_editor:{pane_id}' (RISK-5/MC-5) - typed blocker if a registry rejects it. uuid/chrono are already in the tree (reqwest/others pull them) - do NOT add duplicates.

BACKEND PROOF + HYGIENE + PATHS (MT-008/035 pattern): the emit + flight-recorder-query round-trip + FlightRecorderPane live load are REAL-PG -> bind real + gate behind integration + NEEDS_MANAGED_RESOURCE_PROOF. NativeEditorEvent serde, build_post_body shape, EditorSurfaceRegistry mock-surface callback, error-ring-on-failed-POST (no panic, no frame block), and the surface_extension_seam compile-check are unit-testable STANDALONE. The FlightRecorderPane (flight_recorder_pane.rs, new) async load = no perpetual spinner (MT-015 lesson). NO Color32 literal (theme guard). Screenshots EXTERNAL + assert_no_local_artifact_dir(). FULL suite must stay green (touches save_manager + undo_stack + stage + canvas). ACCESSKIT VARIANT: flight-recorder-pane(Region)/fr-event-{id}(ListItem) - field-correct accesskit 0.21.1. Paths: rich_text.rs->rich_editor/save/save_manager.rs; code_editor.rs->(deferred, code pane unmounted); canvas_board.rs->graph/canvas_board.rs; the bus is interop/interaction_bus.rs (NOT a root event_bus.rs) - co-locate the emitter+registry there or in event_emitter.rs reached via the bus.
ports_from_react: app/src/components/FlightRecorderView.tsx — getEvents, FlightEventFilters, event_type options, filter UI pattern, createDiagnostic for nav failures
app/src/lib/api.ts lines 568-600 — FlightEvent schema, event_type enum, actor field
app/src/lib/api.ts lines 607-617 — RuntimeChatEventV0_1 schema (the POST body format for flight recorder events)
binds_backend_api: POST /api/flight_recorder/runtime_chat_event  =>  api.ts line ~2339 emitRuntimeChatEvent  =>  body: RuntimeChatEventV0_1 { schema_version, event_id, ts_utc, session_id, wsid, ... }
GET /api/flight_recorder  =>  api.ts line 2316 getEvents  =>  params FlightEventFilters { actor, eventType, from, to, wsid }  =>  returns FlightEvent[]
POST /api/diagnostics  =>  api.ts line 841 createDiagnostic  =>  used to log emit failures as diagnostic events when the error ring is full

risks: RuntimeChatEventV0_1 body format mismatch: if the native emitter sends a field name that the backend rejects (e.g. camelCase vs snake_case), the event is silently dropped or the POST returns 422; the flight recorder appears empty even when the editor is active.
tokio::spawn for emit creates unbounded task accumulation if the backend is slow and the editor is very active (e.g. rapid code edits); without backpressure, the spawn queue grows unbounded.
EditorSurface trait object safety: adding a method with a generic parameter later breaks the dyn trait; the initial design must be carefully constrained to object-safe signatures.
surface_extension_seam.rs compiles today but may become stale as other modules evolve (type renames, moved imports); if allowed to drift it becomes misleading documentation.
Actor ID 'native_editor_human' conflicts with existing actor_id conventions in the backend if those are validated against a registry; events may be rejected.
minimum_controls: Write a unit test that serializes NativeEditorEventEmitter.build_post_body() and asserts every required RuntimeChatEventV0_1 field is present with correct snake_case keys; compare against the api.ts schema definition.

Add a semaphore (tokio::sync::Semaphore with permits=20) around spawn in emit(); if all permits are taken, drop the event and log to the in-memory error ring rather than spawning. This bounds concurrency.

Before finalizing the EditorSurface trait, write all expected future method signatures as comments and verify they are object-safe (no generics, no Self by value, no associated types without default); include this check in code review criteria.
Add a CI check: cargo check --all-targets (includes surface_extension_seam.rs); fail the build if it does not compile clean.

Use a descriptive but valid actor_id format: 'hsk:native_editor:{pane_id}' — check backend actor validation rules in src/backend/handshake_core/src before committing to a format; if a registry check exists, add the native editor actor to the allowed set via a backend seeding migration (raise as a typed backend blocker if needed).

verify flight-recorder POST body + closed event_type=system (nest native payload, don't invent type); snake_case exact; Semaphore(20)-bounded spawn; valid actor_id

implementation_result.proofs: AC-4/5/6/7/8 + PT-1/2/5/6 PASS; AC-1/2/3 + PT-3/4 = TYPED BACKEND BLOCKER (honest, not faked); full suite GREEN (1095 lib + all integration, 0 failures); uuid v4 feature enabled (already in tree, no new dep family); no tracked artifacts; FlightRecorderPane proven via AccessKit (wgpu screenshot is GPU-host opt-in).
implementation_result.real_and_wired: NativeEditorEventEmitter + emit_* helpers + RuntimeChatEventV0_1 build_post_body (snake_case, unit-tested) are REAL; LIVE emits wired + proven to reach the transport: emit_document_saved (rich-text save), emit_undo_fired (rich-pane undo dispatch MT-035), emit_route_to_stage (MT-033). Semaphore(20)-bounded spawn + cap-20 ErrorRing on failure (AC-4, no frame block). surface_extension_seam = design-only object-safe EditorSurface trait (AC-5 compiles, contractual). EditorSurfaceRegistry mock-surface callbacks (AC-6). FlightRecorderPane widget (AC-7 AccessKit).
implementation_result.typed_backend_blocker: STRATEGIC FINDING (verified read-only against api/flight_recorder.rs): the FROZEN handshake_core backend CANNOT ingest a native-editor FlightEvent - POST /api/flight_recorder/runtime_chat_event is #[serde(deny_unknown_fields)] (rejects the nested native_editor_event field), CLOSED 3-value type enum (no 'system'), HARDCODES actor_id='runtime_chat', requires non-nil UUID session_id; GET /flight_recorder has NO actor_id filter. So the native-editor observability ROUND-TRIP (AC-1/2/3) needs a NEW backend endpoint (or relaxed schema) - backend work OUTSIDE this frozen-backend WP. Recorded as #[ignore] native_editor_round_trip test (lights up when the backend ships). The client emitter is built+wired+ready. FLAG FOR A FUTURE BACKEND PACKET.
implementation_result.carried_forward_MT_069: emit_code_edit (code pane live edit batch, debounced 2s) + emit_canvas_node_placed (canvas live placement) emit call sites deferred to E11/MT-069 (panes not mounted); helpers READY + unit-tested.
implementation_result.waiver_granted: interop/interaction_bus.rs (emitter+registry fields) + rich_editor/renderer/rich_editor_widget.rs (live emits) + stage_pane.rs + Cargo.toml/lock (uuid v4) + new event_emitter.rs/surface_extension_seam.rs/flight_recorder_pane.rs + test in allowed_paths.
